behaviors.description: The outgoing provider request includes the full taxonomy label list every time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (classifier -> API client payload handoff), not just isolated algorithm logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network is not required; a stub client that captures the dispatched payload can expose missing labels. Rejected System because behavior text is about request contents ('request ... includes ... labels'), not real HTTP/runtime effects. No LLM-DEP or MULTI-STEP.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake provider client, invoke classify() with a taxonomy, and assert the captured outbound request contains every configured label (no drops/reordering bugs from wiring/serialization).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires module wiring to dispatch path; unit-only pure-function test could miss integration serialization/handoff bugs.

behaviors.description: If provider returns a label outside taxonomy, module returns "unknown".
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is response-normalization guard logic and can be validated at one local function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: none needed. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on mapping unexpected value to fallback ('returns "unknown"'), which is local validation logic; cross-module interaction is not required to trigger it. No LLM-DEP or MULTI-STEP.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/validation path with taxonomy + raw label not present in taxonomy; assert output is exactly "unknown".
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A focused unit test directly targets the failure mode and is sufficient.

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw provider responses.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure determinism of normalization step; single local boundary with no I/O. Rejected Integration because no module handoff is required by behavior text ('normalization step always produces the same final label'). No REAL-INFRA, LLM-DEP, or MULTI-STEP.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (or property-test) with identical raw response fixtures and taxonomy; assert identical final label every run.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Determinism check is fully local and does not need wiring.

behaviors.description: Across repeated live API calls with same input, output stays consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on behavior of a real external AI API under repeated calls; mocks would hide model/provider variability. REAL-INFRA is also present, but Agentic is the tighter boundary because model decision variability is central. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-agent/multi-decision pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run multiple real provider calls for identical document+taxonomy; capture raw provider outputs and module labels; assert label is stable whenever raw provider response is unchanged, and only differs when provider response differs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Using the real model/API is necessary; stubbed responses cannot expose live consistency failures.

behaviors.description: Token budget policy is enforced before dispatch (truncate/reject per config).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff: config + budget checker + dispatch gate. A unit test of token math alone can miss bypass bugs in call path. Rejected System because behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement ('before dispatch'); no real network/OS dependency needed. Rejected Agentic because this exclusively tests infrastructure around request construction, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with budget config and fake provider client; pass over-budget input; assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) and that dispatch behavior matches policy (e.g., no send on reject).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs module wiring to prove enforcement occurs on actual dispatch path.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document remains within configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires real provider-reported usage; stubs can mask tokenizer/accounting mismatches between local expectations and provider accounting. Per LLM-DEP rule, passing through real AI/ML API defaults to Agentic even with deterministic assertions. Rejected Integration because mock usage values would not catch real provider accounting drift. Rejected Workflow because this is a single-call budget check, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification on representative document(s), read provider usage metadata from actual responses, and assert reported total tokens <= configured budget.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Only real provider usage metrics can catch this failure class.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit signal, module waits and retries instead of failing immediately.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is retry orchestration across local modules (error mapping, backoff policy, retry loop). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with deterministic fake provider returning rate-limit then success; no OS/network/subprocess-specific behavior required. Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating real transport-level timing/network conditions, only policy ('waits and retries'). Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake provider sequence: first response rate-limited, second success; verify module delays/backoff invocation and eventually returns success rather than propagating first error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration wiring is needed to validate retry loop and state transition end-to-end without real network.
